name: W8A8
oneliner: Both weights and activations quantized to 8-bit — saves memory AND compute via integer arithmetic. Standard for data-center serving.
explanation: Both operands of the matrix multiply are int8, enabling INT8 GEMM with INT32 accumulation. Provides both memory bandwidth savings (2$\times$ vs fp16) and compute savings (INT8 Tensor Cores are faster). Requires quantizing activations at runtime, which is harder due to input-dependent distributions and outliers. Standard for production serving via TensorRT-LLM, vLLM. SmoothQuant makes this viable by mathematically migrating quantization difficulty from activations to weights.
fundamentals: Inference: 1) Quantize activation: X_int8 = round(X_fp16/s_x). 2) Read int8 weights. 3) INT8 GEMM: Y_int32 = X_int8 @ W_int8. 4) Dequantize: Y_fp16 = Y_int32 $\times$ s_x $\times$ s_w. Key difference from W4A16: saves both bandwidth AND compute. For large batch sizes (compute-bound workloads), W8A8 provides real speedup while W4A16 does not.
